source.title: A critical role for the right fronto-insular cortex in switching between central-executive and default-mode networks
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

confidence_rationale: Foundational fMRI + Granger causality study establishing rFIC (anterior insula) and dACC (salience network) as causal initiators of CEN-DMN switching. Replicated across three tasks (auditory segmentation, visual oddball, resting state). Directly licenses SD-032a's architectural framing of a salience-network coordinator that emits mode-switch triggers. Source quality high (PNAS, large influence on downstream literature); mapping fidelity high but not perfect because biology quantifies switching between two networks whereas SD-032a's operating_mode vector spans four modes (external_task, internal_planning, internal_replay, offline_consolidation).
failure_signatures: If the SD-032a coordinator produces coherent mode changes WITHOUT an AIC+dACC aggregate signal exceeding a threshold, the biological grounding is weakened -- the switch should be initiated by a salience-aggregate signal, not by individual subdivisions directly.

If CEN-analogue (task-oriented) and DMN-analogue (default/internal) substrates in ree-v3 can be driven to switch by any single upstream signal without coordinator gating, the Sridharan architecture is violated.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The right fronto-insular cortex, together with dorsal ACC, acts as a causal initiator (via Granger causality) of transitions between the central executive network and the default mode network, across both task and rest conditions.
mapping.ree_translation: The AIC+dACC salience aggregate is the biological analogue of SD-032a's coordinator: it reads multimodal salience signals and emits the mode_switch_trigger that reconfigures downstream networks. In ree-v3 this maps to SalienceCoordinator aggregating dACC PE bundle + drive_level + offline-state into operating_mode and firing the MECH-259 trigger.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Biology: 3 networks (SN, CEN, DMN) with pairwise switching. ree-v3: 4 discrete modes with soft-probability vector. The discrete-mode falsification signature (atomic broadcast at threshold crossing) is an ree-v3 design choice not directly tested in Sridharan; biological switching is graded in continuous BOLD signal. The Granger evidence licenses the ARCHITECTURAL role of AIC as causal initiator, not the specific four-mode discretisation.
mapping.source_context: Healthy adult humans; 3T fMRI; three tasks (auditory event segmentation, visual oddball, resting state); Granger causality analysis + chronometric techniques; unilateral (right) fronto-insular emphasis.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (PNAS, highly cited, replicated widely including by Goulden 2014). Mapping fidelity moderately high -- Sridharan directly supports the coordinator architecture but the ree-v3 four-mode discretisation is an architectural extension. Transfer risk low-moderate: human fMRI to REE substrate is a plausible architectural mapping, though biological SN operates on BOLD timescales (seconds) while ree-v3 coordinator ticks per action. Confidence weighted toward mapping_fidelity since this is an architectural claim.
